ticket_title: Wrong self link returned
ticket_description_snippet: When a stream with Spring XD 1.1.1.RELEASE is created the REST methods always replies """"/stream/_stream-name_"""" as the self link. Unfortunately when this one is called a 404 is returned. So the questions is if this is really intended to work like that. I would expect to get '/stream/definitions/_stream-name_' back instead.  {code:shell} $ curl -F """"name=ticktap"""" -F """"definition=tap:stream:tick > log"""" -F """"deploy=true"""" http://localhost:9393/streams/definitions/ {""""name"""":""...
reasoning: The ticket describes a REST API behavior issue specific to Spring XD 1.1.1.RELEASE, similar to past tickets like XD-3083 (3 SP) where stream/job definition API misuse led to broken behavior and error responses. The current issue is focused on incorrect self-link URLs returned by the API during deployments, which mirrors the scope of those previous bugs regarding API contract inconsistencies. Since this problem centers on REST endpoint response correctness rather than a complex system change or multiple subsystems, it can be estimated at 3 story points.

ticket_title: / by zero in ComponentStatistics
ticket_description_snippet: Recently I have been querying JMX statistics periodically via the MXBean interface (domain=Mule.*, type=org.mule.Statistics). After collecting statistics for each MBean, I reset the bean by invoking """"clearStatistics"""" with no arguments, so that I can come back and collect fresh statistics later.    Howeven, when I do this while mule is under load, I see that the ComponentStatistics class starts to throw an exception on a divide-by-zero error. It tends to happen on multiple processor threads...
reasoning: The ticket describes a persistent divide-by-zero error in Mule's ComponentStatistics during high-load JMX stats collection, similar to MULE-9162 (2 SP) where ForEach components were loaded twice and caused registry issues leading to instability. The current issue is more severe as it involves a critical arithmetic exception that occurs repeatedly under load, likely due to improper state handling in statistics accumulation. Since the root cause appears to be a systemic flaw affecting all high-load stats collection scenarios (not just one component), this ticket's scope is broader than MULE-9162 and may require coordination across multiple subsystems for resolution.

ticket_title: Fit covariance-based ellipse parameters in MultiProFit
ticket_description_snippet: MultiProFit currently fits ellipse shapes using the major axis, axis ratio, position angle parameterization. While easily understood, the position angle is problematic - it is unconstrained for near-circular ellipses and has periodic boundary conditions. This ticket will switch to fitting sigma_x, sigma_y and rho from the covariance matrix [[sigma_x**2, rho*sigma_x*sigma_y], [rho*sigma_x*sigma_y, sigma_y**2]], which is well-behaved and has reasonable limits of sigma_x, sigma_y >= 0 and -1 <= rho...
reasoning: The current ticket modifies MultiProFit's ellipse fitting by switching from the position angle parameterization to a direct fit of sigma_x, sigma_y, and rho using the covariance matrix, which is more numerically stable and avoids the periodicity issues with position angles seen in past tickets like DM-13406 (8 SP) and DM-20265-related changes. Since this change only affects how parameters are extracted from the covariance rather than introducing new fitting algorithms or subsystem interactions, it aligns closely in scope with previous 5–8 point implementations focused on parameterization improvements.
